text: I can't stand T-mobile it's sucks the whole company is awful no one should sighn up with this company me and my dad got 2 iPhones 7+ they were over charging me $700 dolloars for phone service for 1 month I did not know it was this much until I seen my T-Mobile acc and they took the money from my credit card after we were almost done paying off our phones by payments every month it said Our service is  245 for both lines including service I seen they were suspending our service because  the bill was due for $300 and the next month $400 and they said there is $170 and ressurciring charges and misc charges of +$70 charges and plus $20 each when you don't pay the bill but I called up before this all happend and asked for an extension then I was late a few days and they still charged me and when I called up they said it was a Error and there sorry I was not suppose to be charged that much and restored Prut service finally I was like yay👍🏼And then we keep getting charged over and over for no reason finally they have put our service back on and seen it was there fault but we have been paying so much extra money and be takes an advantage . So I'm convincing my dad we must switch to Metro PCS  they but out your contract pay your phone off and you just have to switch to them so I hope to be leaving this horrible T-mobile and i recomand for no one to ever sighn up with t-mobile the internet is horrible everything about there company is horrible 👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻

Hi, I live in Ga & I'm so sick of T- mobile they have horrible customer service! I hate this company! I will be leaving soon going somewhere else!

Sad to say but I wish I can give them negative stars !!! Worst customer service and no one ever knows what they tell you!!!!!hate t mobile !!!!!will never go back since today 09-07-27
They stold extra money from me again and this is their response at all at least u don't owe" after I always pay !!!

I will never buy another t mobile phone!!!! I have been with them for the last 10 years, and put up with the spotty cellphone service,  because it was a little cheaper family plan. 
 Today they made a offer where you buy one phone get one free... I already have four phone on this plan , so I called hoping we could upgrade our phones.  I was told if I added another phone line I could! Forget you t mobile!

Horrible experience! I am in Vancouver right now. I cannot call or text my family in California because it is blocked. I cannot reach Customer Service because TMobile  blocked that too.  Before I left California, I changed my plan from pay as you go (average $40/month) to a monthly pay with data ($45) and additional $15 for international texting. So for $60, I get ZERO service. How's that for nickling and diming? Beyond frustrating.

I hate the fact that even you are with T Mobile for more than 2 years and paying your bills monthly, they still checking your credit score if you want to upgrade your phone!! So sad :(
I hate the fact that even you are with T Mobile for more than 2 years and paying your bills monthly, they still checking your credit score if you want to upgrade your phone!! So sad :(

I have been with the company since 2008 and I  they have changed for the worse. They use to care about their customers, but now they only care for their profits. I have had issues with Samsung phones twice on less than 6 months!!! To get a replacement is rare without shelling out another couple hundred dollars. 
The service is poor and does not work in areas locally and when traveling. The Samsung phones are having problems but They do not notify customers or they run diagnostics that cause your phone crash then offer nothing but dishonesty and too concerned with placing blame then they tell you "you can always upgrade. Just pay $185 to get a phone today. " Sounds like a BIG SCAM TO ME!$!! They find ways to get out of warranties, the so called insurance, and accountability of misinforming customers!!! I also asked for a superior, Igor, and he was so much worse than the reps!! Condesending, arrogant...but they care about customers? Doesn't sound like it to me! I would never recommend this company to anyone! Save your money, time and effort for a company who cares about customer satisfaction!

Absolute worst tech-support I've ever had anywhere for anything first of all it's all offshore in the Philippines bad enough you can't understand every other word that spoken to you the people that you're speaking with our complete idiots either that or they're just extremely poorly trained  whatever the case there's no excuse I have had nothing but technical problems since I opened up the service a little over a month ago you would think that after a month they have all the bugs worked out but they don't this is the absolute worst technical nightmare I have ever experienced avoid T-Mobile at all costs

Wish this site had minus sign...first and foremost I been with this lame azz company for years believing that the serious was going to get better. I work in the chauffeur world and my phone never worked in the airport. Called numerous times to fix the matter and complain they said they was working on the matter. Months went by even a whole year phone never worked write if I wanted it not to work I would have stayed with spint... anyways as a result went to verizon and never have any problems and I called t mobile to cut off my lines off paid my last bill and they said it would be cut off after the end of billing cycle... these Mfs billed me again afterwards saying the other phones was still on after they was supposed to be cut off. I paid they lame azz amount so I wouldn't fcked up my credit in my future purchases. At the end don't use this company they are bad news, all they do is lie and never did cht. Fck t mobile i will not ever get this company again. Nor will I refer a friend or family member and if y'all read this I would advise you to leave them or not get them either.. mock my words you will regret it. I guess at the end you really get what you paid for and that is chitty service and a bunch of lies. Never again t mobile never again

I have been long time customer with Tmobile and I've tolerated their poor customer service numerous times when I could have taken my business elsewhere I continue as a Tmobile customer however I certainly regret that I stayed with Tmobile this longtime  and will be taken my business elsewhere there customer service representatives need training in accuracy I contacted Tmobile on Friday August 4th and made a customer service representative fully aware of due to a financial difficulty that I could not meet my pre-arranged payment agreement and that I could make the payment on Wednesday August 9th the representative place me on a lengthy hold came back on the phone line and say exact words no problem Wednesday August 9th will be fine and ask what time I could make payment I said in the morning the representative said no worries I'll give you (me) until Wednesday August 9th evening I asked not once but twice will this affect my phone services or cause a interruption in my services I was advised twice by this representative no my services will not be interrupted and that my account had a promise to pay after phone conversation I assume everything was handled correctly per the Tmobile representative I spoke with however on Sunday August 6th my phone services were interrupted I called tmobile spoke to a representative that sounded like a script and said that unfortunately I can not extend payment til Wednesday was placed on another lengthy hold representative came back on phone and said they understand what I was advised but basically that can not be done so I ask for a supervisor that said that I was passed 30 days and that he could give me to Monday at midnight to pay the amount and he'll restore my services neither of the representatives seem to understand the fact of what I was advised Friday August 4th I've worked in customer service for many years and know the customer isn't always right but when they are they are right and when a company makes a mistake it is honored however the supervisor said his system only allowed him to offer me the extention til Monday August 7th at midnight ask him why was it on Friday customer service representative advise me that making payment on Wednesday August 9th was fine no problem but today August 6th it's impossible system will not allow the supervisor couldn't explain and said that he will send some message to the representative I spoke to Friday and advised I was informed incorrectly I told him I work in customer service myself and that informing the representative of their mistake doesn't fix what the customer was informed and that the company should  make it right with the customer especially a long time customer all he could say was he apologize he'll restore my services until Monday at midnight and waive restore fees I said that doesnt suffice for what I was informed and again I can not make payment til Wednesday August 9th he then transferred me to Customer Loyalty Department which was a cold transfer so to add more fuel to my fire I had to start over with a new representative as to what's going on then apparently she saw in the system the hold the representative put on my account Friday August 4th but advised me it was done incorrectly so I said so that's my fault as a customer she said no but now my account is 31 days past due if I would of called Saturday August 5th she said the hold could've been placed properly I said WHAT! how the heck was I suppose to know that representative did their job incorrectly she said she knows but now the system ( yet again) will not allow a hold to be placed on account I said  when a company representative does something incorrect or advise a customer incrrectly it's the company job to make it right not the customers fault she said she understood and placed me on a lengthy hold just to say if once again I would called on Saturday August 5th the hold could've been done correctly and now the system will not allow and that she spoke to her supervisor supposedly  that can't get around the the system either. I want what I was advised and that's to extend my payment to Wednesday August 9th tmobile & their understanding the customer is horrible I've been with Tmobile a long time and this isn't the first time I experienced their unorthodox customer service but this is yet the worse they need training from manager's to the representatives how to correctly handle customer's and to honor what they advise a customer no computer system is more powerful than a human but apparently to Tmobile customer service it is I want what I was advisedl

My husband and I switched to Tmobile last March and were promised a rebate for an early termination fee for a contract with Verizon. I submitted for the rebate in March/17 and never heard back from them. I called last week (7/17) to check status only to find out that it had been denied due to my not submitting the correct paperwork (5/17). T mobile never contacted me regarding the mistake. I was then told that after getting the correct paperwork I would be able to resubmit for the rebate, however when I tried to do this I was denied. After calling Customer Service and being put on hold again and again I found that they still could not help me. I eventually spoke with a supervisor who again could not help me unless my husband authorized the rebate submission. I repeatedly told them that he was out of state in business meetings and they kept saying over and over "we will just contact the account holder for authorization". I BLEW! The reps were just reading off a script and not listening to what I was telling them. This all appears to be a big run around. Will not be renewing my contract.

They would not honor a phone warranty.
Bad customer service.

Yesterday I went into my local Ferndale, Wa T-Mobile store and was wanting to purchase a new phone the associates were very helpful and kind and directed me to the kind of phone that I wanted.

I am currently on the phone right now with tmobile. I was on the phone with them for 2 hours. They keep transferring me to different people regarding my account. Im on hold for 40 minutes for a supervisor. Before, Two different supervisors told me my account wouldnt be suspended and it has been twice. I already made an arrangement with them and i keep having to call back asking to restore my account. Tmobile is so disorganized and a mess. Now the lady who was on the phone with me before is telling me that my account can be suspended anytime from today until friday when i specifically asked if my account will be suspended until my arrangement is due and was told it wouldnt be until my arrangement is due. I understand you should pay your bills on time but ultimately im paying restoration and prorated fees for each line for having to restore my line. As soon as I get a chance I am ending my business with tmobile and moving to another more better carrier.

I have been with this company for years! Now once I have been told I wouldn't have a bill until Aug to pay after passing my bill in June, now I'm stuck having to pay for a bill this month.  I was told by two other reps about doing this and now they are saying I have to pay this bill!! T mobile had been decent so far except for the Indian reps they have who cannot understand you and what your needing help with! Now I'm going to look for another company to switch to especially after this incident!!

The WORST service of anyone I've ever had to deal with. I've been having issues with them for 10 months now, including a claim with the Better Business Bureau. I had no idea a company could be worse than Comcast. Do NOT get tricked into their unlimited data plan. NOT worth the hassle and insane hidden fees. I've never had to deal with any company like this before. They constantly hang up when "transferring" me to another department. TERRIBLE COMPANY.

Pros: Fastest internet within city limits. Customer service rep gave additional GBs for trouble.

Cons: Sign up process is not thought through. They send activation codes via SMS to "data only" sims. Payment refill system doesn't work "unavailable". Troubleshooting team doesn't really understand English well, attempting to convey issue is complete a waste of time, tenacity doesn't prove useful.

This is the worst cellphone company, I do not recommend anyone to share with them, even if they offered you a free line, because then they will steal you by deceiving you and take unwarranted amounts from the start. I do not have an international communication service. From the start, when my son played on the phone and made an international phone call, even though I am not a subscriber to the international call service, they added $ 134 to the monthly bill. When we contacted the customer service, they never helped us and also offered to share with them and take IPhone 7 is free to give them your old phone this offer all Lie with them, and after they did not pay socialist the full amount of the company and the old was vorizen and the old company Talaptna to pay $ 200 increase because the company did not t mobil pay them the full amount.

The guy helping me was super nice, but the customer service he was receiving while on the phone with T-Mobile was terrible. I waited for 50 minutes trying to switch over from AT&T, but the rep on the phone couldn't help him activate the new plan because of the error he made.  50 minutes later, customer service couldn't resolve the issue so I decided to stick with AT&T. CS at T-Mobile is terrible.

I had the pleasure of working with a gentleman named Dakota (5116) in the technology help department. My phone had stopped working and would not go past the welcome screen. Dakota took his time and walked me through every little step, he made it extremely painless. I got on the phone in the worst mood and got off of the phone laughing with a fixed phone. Thank you Dakota for making my experience so easy and making my day do a complete 360!

-Kelsey

So I had decided to switch from Verizon to T-Mobile on Saturday @ 12pm because everyone was making such a big deal about the unlimited data plans! 1st the customer service rep was totally clue less when it come to making the switch and did not provide me with adequate instruction or steps to correctly complete the porting process. Needless to say by 10pm that same day i could not send or receive text messages, I could not make or receive calls because the coverage was horrible! I called into to customer service and once again they were totally clueless, they couldn't not trouble shoot my phone after 1 hr of trying. So i immediate asked for the port to be cancelled and to be switched back to Verizon. Because of their lack of common sense and understanding of their own processes i had to go 2 days without service and had to depend solely on Verizon to assist me with saving my number and providing me with a temporary option to get me through these past 2 nightmare of days. T-Mobile Never again! Although the internet was nice, the services and customer service was horrible.

I have been with Tmobile for 10 years. I have Unlimited everything, which costs  $104.28 per month for one phone. Approximately 6-8 months ago, I get these messages telling me that I've reached my 8 g's. They use the words "push". I'm supposed to have 30 g's not 8. Every representative I speak to explains it away, but I'm not buying it. My experience with the representatives are always nice, but this slow down is crazy. Today I let my battery drain completely, now it's been 4 hours charging and it's still not 100%. I can't even update my apps. I wasn't even able to access the Tmobile Tuesday app today because it's not updated. My g's aren't updated until the 11th. Last month my phone speed was still slow after 6am on the 11th. The representatives (2) told me my g's were reset, but I didn't see any changes. My smartphone is about 8 months old. I don't like changing carrier's especially since there really isn't any other carriers I like. 😣😢

I will have been with The Mobile for a year in July. It started out a little rough but I am completely happy with this company. ANY issue I have had they have fixed. If you want any thing fixed MAKE SURE YOU GET AN AMERICAN if you want to get someone that really understands you in English. I have had issues and gotten representatives that English is not their first language and they never fix the issues. I hang up on anyone that doesn't speak English well when I hear them and keep recalling until I get someone that does understand me. That is the only issue I've ever had with T Mobile. I don't care if I have to keep calling and hanging up until I get someone that understands me because it's worth it to me to get my issue(s) fixed. I love that on Tuesdays customers get cool deals and free stuff. They don't have to do it but they do and I pray they don't merge with Sprint because I HATED Sprint. 
As for now I have my bill directly taken out of my bank monthly and my bill has actually went down in price by 10 dollars since I started with TMobile. I love this company so much and I can't say it enough. All I have to say is if you have and issue, you haven't spoken with someone that speaks your language correctly or you have someone that is not having a good day. My suggestion is call someone else for customer service until you get your issue taken care of. I'm not joking, they will really work with you if you put in the time to find the right person to speak with. I have been an AT&T customer, a Cricket customer, a Sprint customer, and now a TMOBILE customer. I can tell you right now that Tmobile has done more for me then any  of the ones I have been with. I will be a TMOBILE customer for life if they keep doing what they are doing right now. As I've said I pray they don't merge with Sprint. Sprint customer service is THE WORST I've ever experienced and they gave me a lot of problems more than any company I've ever been with and Cricket a very close second. I have nothing but good things to say about Tmobile and I plan to stay with them as long as they keep thinking about their customers first as they do right now. If by chance they merge with Sprint, I pray TMOBILE makes Sprint conform to what we customers have with TMOBILE. As I've said Sprint is the worst company I've ever dealt with and I left them went to Cricket and then came to TMOBILE and I will stay with TMOBILE because to them it's all about the customers. It is NOT about the customers with the others. Again I want to say I love you TMobile!!! <3 <3 <3

I ported 3 numbers over to add to my existing Tmoblie account from Verizon to save money and take advantage of a promotion they offered to receive a Samsung Galaxy S7 free when you trade in a device and come from another network. I traded in 2 New Motorola G4 pluses and got my Samsungs. On the next bill they charged me for the phones. I called to fix the error and was told that my trade-ins didn't qualify for the promotion. Even though the store took the phones. I was never given a credit of any kind for the trade-ins. I have been trying to resolve the issue with their Loyalty department for the last 2 frustrating months. I was told I would get a call back at the end of April with a resolution. No call. 2 weeks later and I'm calling then back. Every time I call its the same thing, I talk to a customer service rep who asks me to tell the story all over again (even though they supposedly take notes on the account) then they transfer me to a Loyalty specialist who asks me to go over the story again. This morning I've been on the phone with them for over 2 hours. I've been disconnected twice and received no call back (even though they ask for a call back number in case you get disconnected) , had to call them and yes go over the story all over again.  I still have no resolution. They said they will not give me credit of any kind. T Mobile stole my Motorolas and conned me into buying 2 Samsungs that retail for over $1100.  How is it possible to be this incredibly inept? I used to think Comcast had the worst customer service. I was wrong. I'm using my tax return to pay off the Samsungs and porting back to Verizon and I will never, ever use Tmobile again.

my name is mike. I HAVE BEEN OVERCHARGED ON BILL 3 DIFFRENT TIMES AND EVERY TIME YOU CALL THEM THEY DON'T DO ANY THING ABOUT IT. I WAS CHARGED OVER $ 400 EXTRA ON MY LAST BILL FOR INTERNATIONAL CALL BUT I HAD INTENATIONAL PLAN WHICH CHARGES $ 15 A MONTH TO COVER INTERNATIONAL CALLS.
I WAS TOLD THAT SOME ONE REMOVED THAT FEATURE FROM MY PLAN  BUT NO ONE CALLED OR USED MY PHONE TO DROP THAT FEATURE. THEY ARE NOT ABLE TO PROVE THAT WHO CANCELED OR CALLED OR USED AN APP OR THE INTERNET TO CANCEL. THE FACT IS NO ONE CALLED TO CANCEL AND I WAS CHARGED REGULAR PRICE FOR THE INTERNATIONAL CALLS. I HAD THAT FEATURE ON MY PLAN FOR THE LAST 6 MONTHS. THEY CHRAGED 
OVER $ 400 EXTRA AND WANT TO GIVE ME CREDIT FOR $ 100 ONLY. I AM GOING TO SWITCH THE TO ANOTHER COMPANY. THIS IS A VERY BAD /THIEF COMPANY. I WILL NOT DEAL WITH THIS COMPANY AGAIN.

I went to T-Mobile to buy a cheap top up plan for 30-days. They didn't have any top up packages that lasted 30 days, so the best I could do was the "$30 Tourist Plan". It cost me $42 plus change. Twenty-one days later, I lost all service. I went back to T-Mobile and waited nearly an hour (even though there were 3 employees and only one customer ahead of me). After all that time, they told me the plan I bought was only a 21-day plan. The other guy had lied straight to my face. They refused to rectify the situation and said that I must pay if I want service for the remaining 9 days. I refused, went home, and checked my original paperwork. Nowhere in the paperwork does it say the length of the plan! Why? The only reason would be to trick people into having to pay for additional service. They're ripping off their own customers!

I had an issue with my bill. I am a new customer, and my bill included something that per current promotion should be waived. It took an hour and a half on the phone with Customer Service - was hung up on twice (once by a manager who never called back) for them to tell me that their Store made promises that were untrue and that the promotion worked differently than explained. 

I was not using bad language, although by my 5th transfer I was not very happy (manager was the 6th person I spoke with). People 1-4 all said something was wrong with my bill but they were transferring me to the department that could help. Person 5 said I was just misinformed, and explained how the promotion worked. Which was not what I was told or agreed to in store, but at least I understood. When the manager got on the phone as I was unhappy with my time of resolution, we got "disconnected" - I was hung up on - again. 

Worst customer service I've ever experienced. Transferred from Verizon - long time customer. Am now considering cutting my loses, paying the disconnect fee, and going back to Verizon. After a month.

Unacceptable service and coverage for pre paid mobile sim card users in Montenegro.

All tourists, if you travel to Montengro use another company, as soon as you put money on T mobile pre paid sim card, the company automatically withdraws credit and upon contacting them, they simply explain the reason being you having applications running in the background which used the credit. 
They use this as a standard excuse, even though the internet connection is off on the mobile and all apps closed, they still manage to use this poor excuse as explanation. 
The service is extremely poor, both on phone and on site, the people working in the shops are poorly educated in customer service if any, the lack of sheer sense of helping and being of service is non existent.
They act arrogant and like they are doing you a favor for even working there :) 
No problems can be solved over phone, and you will be asked to go to one af the shops and waste your vacation time, and the inconvinience due to their lack of profesionalism and cabability.

Dear people, you have been warned.

Good day!

USELESS
We bought 2 brand new phones. The phones worked for about 10 days. We cannot make or receive calls the phone says emergency calls only. I called the store, and was told both phones needed new SIM cards, and that the SIM cards would be waiting for us when we got the there the next day 03/31/17. We get to the store, no one knew anything they reset the phones, told us it is due to where we live. When we got home we tried to use the phones again, same problem. I called the store (from my landline) Only to have the store Manager tell me to come in and get a different phone for more $$. We will be going there today to return the phones since we are within the 14 day return period. STAY AWAY from T-Mobile! Phones purchased 03/18/17

A sales rep named Laura A. met with me in West Haven, CT last year.  She gave me all false information (to get me to sign on with them) saying they would pay off my Sprint termination fees.  I would only have to pay $103.00 for my phones and to activate my service.  So of course I signed on with them.  2 days after signing I just happened to look at the charges on my credit card. The one I used to pay  T-Mobile.  I was charged over $1,000 for the phones and then another $300.00 to activate my service. I also found out they were in no way going to pay off my Sprint account. Needless to say I cancelled immediately. I ended up returning the phone's unopened. I called my creditcard company and they cancelled the 2 transactions when i showed them the contract i had signed stated i was to pay $103.00.  A few months later they put me in collections saying I owed them over $2000.00. They suck!!  And so does that sales rep. She was the biggest liar!!

I've been a T-Mobile customer for 2 years. For Black Friday they advertised a special to upgrade an iPhone 6 to an iPhone 7. After talking to a T-Mobile agent at their store I traded in my phone under the clear confirmation that my current plan did not have to change. 2 weeks later T-Mobile sent me a text message stating that I was no longer eligible for the deal unless I changed my plan, which would cost $30 more per month. I called customer service and was told that nothing could be done and my bill would increase no matter what I did and that I could not return the iPhone 7 and get my 6 back. They also admitted that they were looking into the issue of many customers having the same experience of being told one thing in the store and another weeks later through a text message, but even then they were not willing to correct or make the situation right. I used to like this company and encouraged my friends to switch from thier plans to them. This is no longer the case. I now feel like they little regard for appearing honest and trust worthy to their customers.
We used T-Mobile for more than 2 years. They extended our contract without consulting us first, and when we chose to give up their service they said we need to pay the taxes.

I must have accidentally short paid my bill last month by $0.02. I received a threatening text alert that my payment ($0.02) was due by the next business day or my services would be canceled. I immediately used the link to make my payment. The minimum amount they accept is $1.01. So they owe me $0.90 and an apology for being jerks and threatening a good customer.
Sold me a locked Iphone that I now can't use when moved to another country... Paid full price.. No 2-year contract or anything like that. Thanks! $550 for nothing!

T-mobile welcomes you with a red carpet when you sign on their service. Once you are their regular customer they flash their backsides. AVOID them like the HIV AIDS virus/Ebola virus. My 14 year old son who had a issue with a most expensive Samsung device made a disparaging statement about their attitude (He made a remark that the modern day companies do not act like this anymore). The senior company store personnel talk like payday lenders. Take my sincere advice do not sign on their service, better still dump them like poison!
T mobile has good packages and inexpensive rates. Unfortunately the coverage is not great. The customer service is just ok: average for a mobile phone company.

I had been with T Mobile for a good many years and still a customer with them until I can find a mobile broadband service for pay-as-go service for a USB dongle - no contracts or land-line phones included.

I have no idea why T Mobile eradicated my Web and Walk account with them but this occurred after I purchased a replacement USB PAYGO Internet dongle in Sept/Oct 2014. There was £22.00 on my account I cannot access or retrieve from the company, and tried a plethora of consumer-advice related things to get them to refund my money or at the very least, place the credit onto my new web N Walk account - I don't use these accounts in the main because I Top Up my account via a Top-UP card associated with it.

They still have my money and still closed my former/existing web n walk account without any consultation with me.   They are scrupulous and must be investigated by OFGEM - My complaint even with them never amounted to anything either.
As everyone knows they provide terrible mobile service and worst customer support. There is no coordination between their different departments which caused customer a lot of difficulties.
I never have signal, what good is a phone without any signal!! I've been trying to send a text and it can't be sent.  BAD SERVISE!!
I never have signal, what good is a phone without any signal!! I've been trying to send a text and it can't be sent.  BAD SERVISE!!

I switched from Verizon to T-mobile on September 2014. They have advertisement about paying your ETF and will reimburse it within 8 weeks. But it will take longer than 8 weeks. All there agents even the supervisor will give you a FALSE information about the status. They will tell you it was mailed out already and take 15 "DAYS" (not working/business days). After 15 days it did not arrive on the mail. Called them back again and told me it was mailed out yesterday and gave me expectations of 15 "DAYS" (not working/business days). I checked the website swithc2t-mobile.com and my etf was approved on 11/12/14 "Should receive it within 2-3 weeks." But no ETF reimbursement came in. I'm tired of telling me 15 "DAYS" (not working/business days) and delay etc. 

About the service... Not so good as Verizon. I'm within the city with 4G LTE coverage but went inside store like Targe/Fred Meyer and other. "NO SERVICE" on my iPhone.

I was with T-Mobile for around 10 years without a hitch but fell into a trap when trying to arrange additional contracts.  I was promised good deals if I brought in additional family members after renewing my contract.  This promise was refused but by that time I was stuck on another 2 year cycle.  I left as soon as the contract ended.

The service is robust and has good coverage, but this appears to have gone to their heads as they assume that they are essential to everyone.

I'm an insurance agent.  We were working on applications for the Obama Care from October till March 31st.  For the entire months, I would buy a hotspot refill card for 50.00 - 4g speed - 5gb.  The largest amount possible sold at Wal-Mart.

During those months, I would re-buy a card for my company every 2 weeks.  Then on the last 2 days (WHICH 1 DAY I COULDN"T EVEN LOG ONTO healthcare.gov site), 50.00 was used up each day.

I called in to complain and they refused to see what was happening.  THERE IS NO WAY that I USED 50.00 of Hotspot worth in 5 hours each day - equaling 100.00!  especially when 1 day I couldn't even go to the site I was needing to use.

It is a HORRIBLE company.  I REGRET the day I bought the hotspot from them.  Pay a little more with At & T and you know where you stand with your plan.

They are JUST horrible!

I am annoyed with T-Mobile right now, they recently expanded international plans so it's great when you travel buy they got rid of a lot of calling discounts for different countries... I wound up with $60 of overages on my last bill because they failed to inform me. they did credit it but I'm still annoyed cause now I need to use Skype to call my family back home.
